briefing.title: Strong Volume Lead, But Value Gap vs. Peers
briefing.detail: Purse Maison leads all stores in units sold MTD (204) and sold value (₱44.46M), yet our avg ticket of ₱174,335 sits well below premium competitors Shop with K (₱325,987) and KC Luxury (₱316,058), signaling a mix skewed toward lower-priced SKUs.
briefing.action: Audit the 'Other Other' and 'Chanel Other' categories driving volume to identify if margin compression is coming from entry-level SKUs — consider throttling intake of sub-₱100K pieces and redirecting OB toward exotic/rare Hermès.

briefing.title: Miss Manila Luxe: High-Velocity Low-Ticket Threat
briefing.detail: Miss Manila Luxe sold 579 units MTD at avg ₱47,714 with a 9.7-day avg sell time — their 74.3% 'Other' mix and fast churn signal aggressive discounting or entry-level targeting that could steal budget-conscious buyers from our ₱174K avg range.
briefing.action: Do not compete on price in the sub-₱100K segment; instead, reinforce positioning around authentication, condition grading, and Hermès depth to hold the ₱150K–₱500K sweet spot.

briefing.title: Hermès Birkin & Kelly Are Market-Wide Top Sellers
briefing.detail: Birkin and Kelly appear in the top 3 sold models at 6 of 8 competitor stores MTD; Purse Maison's Hermès is 39.3% of inventory (₱113.8M estimated value) and both models appear in our top sold list — this is our strongest category.
briefing.action: Prioritize sourcing additional Birkin 25 and Kelly 28 (especially entry-price hardware variants) to sustain sell-through momentum; do not let live count drop below 5 units per size.

briefing.title: Birkin 30 Lowest Ask Needs Urgent Review
briefing.detail: Our Birkin 30 lowest ask is ₱728,000 vs. market lowest of ₱520,000 — a ₱208,000 (40%) premium on entry price with 17 competitor units live; our 4 live units risk stagnation if buyers price-check across platforms.
briefing.action: Review the 1–2 lowest-condition Birkin 30 units: if B/C condition, reprice to ₱580,000–₱620,000 to re-enter competitive range; retain exotic or pristine units at current ask.

briefing.title: Kelly 28 Inventory Depth vs. Aggressive Market
briefing.detail: We hold 10 Kelly 28 units (lowest ask ₱735,000) against a market with 27 live listings and a floor of ₱480,000 — we are ₱255,000 above the market floor with the heaviest stock position among hero models.
briefing.action: Segment the 10 units by leather/hardware: push 2–3 entry-condition pieces to ₱530,000–₱580,000 immediately; hold exotic/gold-HW units at premium and create a featured listing to justify price differential.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo: Largest Single-Model Exposure
briefing.detail: 25 live Jumbo units represent our biggest single-model stock block (lowest ask ₱188,000 vs. market floor ₱170,000) in a 40-unit market — sitting ₱18,000 above floor with heavy supply on both sides.
briefing.action: Run a 7-day Jumbo clearance: reprice the 3–5 oldest-intake units to ₱172,000–₱175,000 to capture price-sensitive buyers without cratering the tier, and set a restock pause until live count falls to ≤15.

alerts: KC Luxury Bags: 154 units, ₱48.67M inventory value, ZERO sales MTD — either very new to tracking or in a hard stall; monitor for sudden price dumps that could pressure Hermès and exotic pricing market-wide.

Luxury Wish has only 168 units yet sold ₱18.02M MTD (avg ticket ₱643,393 per sale vs stated ₱304,631 avg ticket — implied high-value one-off transactions); watch for ultra-premium Birkin/Kelly transactions pulling aspirational buyers from our range.

Mini Kelly (Kelly 20): our lowest ask ₱1,540,000 is ₱570,000 above market floor of ₱970,000 with only 4 units market-wide — investigate if our units are exotic/rare; if not, repricing risk is significant on this illiquid model.

The Bag Hub is moving Hermès Picotin in top sold — a model absent from our top sellers; Picotin's lower price point (typically ₱80K–₱150K) could fill a volume gap between our Hermès tier and entry-level Chanel.

A&J Luxury is selling Hermès Lindy (avg ticket ₱92,567 store-wide) — another accessible Hermès model we are not visibly capitalizing on; consider adding 3–5 Lindy units to broaden Hermès funnel without diluting Birkin/Kelly margins.

pricing.suggestion: Hold current floor at ₱1,288,000 — only ₱8,000 above market low of ₱1,280,000 with a thin 14-unit market; no markdown needed, but resist any new intake priced above ₱1,800,000 unless hardware is exotic.

Reprice the 1–2 lowest-condition units from ₱728,000 down to ₱580,000–₱620,000 to close the 40% gap vs. market floor of ₱520,000; retain pristine/exotic pieces at ₱700,000+ with detailed condition notes.

Our floor ₱1,150,000 is ₱380,000 above market low of ₱770,000 — audit condition: B-condition units should be repriced to ₱850,000–₱950,000; reserve ₱1,150,000+ floor strictly for A/A+ condition with full set.

Segment immediately: move 2–3 entry-condition units to ₱530,000–₱560,000 (vs. market floor ₱480,000), hold mid-tier at ₱680,000–₱720,000, and list top-condition/exotic at ₱900,000+ with premium justification in listing copy.

Constance 18 floor ₱695,000 vs. market ₱650,000 — acceptable 7% premium if condition warrants; Constance 24 floor ₱534,500 vs. market ₱395,000 is a 35% gap — reprice 1–2 units to ₱420,000–₱450,000 to stimulate MTD sell-through on this slower size.

With 25 live units and a market of 40, reprice the 5 longest-aged Jumbos to ₱172,000–₱178,000 (market floor ₱170,000), maintain ₱188,000+ on recent intake with full accessories, and pause new Jumbo acquisitions until stock drops to ≤15 units.
